title: Facilities Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Work-order routing, maintenance scheduling, and energy/space reporting are meaningfully automatable, but vendor negotiation, safety judgment, and on-site building operations are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: Smart-building IoT platforms and AI-driven maintenance-prediction tools are credible and improving quickly, though not yet universal across building stock.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is growing in large commercial portfolios and tech-forward campuses but remains uneven across older or smaller facilities.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can plausibly own ticket triage, preventive-maintenance scheduling, and energy-anomaly flagging; the physical response and vendor relationship layer stays human.

tasksAtRisk: Work order intake, routing, and status tracking
Energy usage monitoring and anomaly reporting
Space utilization data collection and reporting
tasksGrowing: Workplace experience design for hybrid teams
Sustainability program leadership and ESG reporting
Smart building technology integration and vendor oversight
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 11-3013 (Facilities Managers); range reflects single-site/mid-portfolio facilities management, below the senior multi-site tail of the occupation

defenseSkills.skill: Smart Building IoT & Predictive Maintenance Platforms

insight: AI now triages work orders and flags maintenance needs before a person notices, but someone still has to stand in the mechanical room, manage the contractor, and own the safety call. Facilities managers who add sustainability and smart-building program leadership to core operations are growing in scope, not shrinking.
